title: keteleeria davidiana var. sacra
intro: Keteleeria davidiana var. sacra, also known as Korean fir or Korean sacred fir, is a variant of the Keteleeria davidiana tree species native to the Korean Peninsula. This evergreen conifer has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Keteleeria davidiana var. sacra include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as exhibiting potential anti-cancer and neuroprotective effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and needles of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
